title: नरसिंह कवचम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री नरसिंह कवचम् ॥

॥ अथ श्री नरसिंह कवचम् ॥

श्रीनारदोवाच —
इन्द्रियाणां मनोनाथो मनोनाथस्तु मारुतः।
मारुतस्य प्राणनाथो नृसिंहो मे गतिर्भवेत्॥ १॥

श्रीनृसिंहो मे शिरः पातु लोकरक्षार्थसम्भवः।
सर्वगोऽपि स्तम्भवासः फालं मे रक्षतु ध्वनिम्॥ २॥

नृसिंहो मे दृशौ पातु सोमसूर्याग्निलोचनः।
स्मृतिं मे पातु नृहरिर्मुनिवार्यस्तुतिप्रियः॥ ३॥

नासां मे सिंहनासस्तु मुखं लक्ष्मीमुखप्रियः।
सर्वविद्याधिपः पातु नृसिंहो रसनां मम॥ ४॥

वक्त्रं पात्वन्धकारिश्च जिह्वां मे सरसीरुहः।
मन्त्रकण्ठो ध्वनिं पातु नृसिंहो मे भुजद्वयम्॥ ५॥

करौ मे देवराजश्च नखैरालिढमूर्तिमान्।
हृदयं योगिसाध्यश्च निवासं पातु मे हरिः॥ ६॥

मध्यं पातु हिरण्याक्षवक्षःकूटविदारणः।
नाभिं मे पातु विश्वम्भरो दिव्यसिंहो विभूतिदः॥ ७॥

ब्रह्माण्डकोटयः कट्यां यस्योदरगताः कृशः।
स मे कटिं पातु नित्यं दिव्यसिंहो महाबलः॥ ८॥

ऊरू मे पातु भद्रश्री जानुनी नरकान्तकः।
जङ्घे पातु धराधीशो पादौ मे भक्तवत्सलः॥ ९॥

सर्वतोऽप्यखिलं दुर्गं नृसिंहः पातु सर्वदा।
आत्मनः सकलान् क्लेशान् हन्ति सर्वत्र सर्वदा॥ १०॥

इमं नृसिंहकवचं प्रह्लादमुखमण्डितम्।
भक्तिमान् यः पठेन्नित्यं सर्वपापैः प्रमुच्यते॥ ११॥

पुत्रवान् धनवान् लोके दीर्घायुरुपजायते।
कामयते यं यं कामं तं तं प्राप्नोत्यसंशयम्॥ १२॥

सर्वत्र जयमाप्नोति सर्वत्र विजयं लभेत्।
भूम्यन्तरिक्षदिवानां ग्रहाणां विनिवारणम्॥ १३॥

॥ इति श्री ब्रह्माण्डपुराणे प्रह्लादोक्तं श्री नरसिंह कवचम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Narasimha Kavacham ||

|| Atha Shri Narasimha Kavacham ||

Shri Naradovacha —
Indriyanam mano-natho mano-nathas-tu marutah.
Marutasya prana-natho Nrisimho me gatir-bhavet. || 1 ||

Shri Nrisimho me shirah patu loka-rakshartha-sambhavah.
Sarvago-api stambha-vasah phalam me rakshatu dhvanim. || 2 ||

Nrisimho me drishaw patu Soma-Surya-Agni-lochanah.
Smritim me patu Nriharir-muni-varya-stuti-priyah. || 3 ||

Nasam me Simha-nasas-tu mukham Lakshmi-mukha-priyah.
Sarva-vidyadhipah patu Nrisimho rasanam mama. || 4 ||

Vaktram patv-andhakarish-cha jihvam me Sarasiruah.
Mantra-kantho dhvanim patu Nrisimho me bhuja-dvayam. || 5 ||

Karau me Deva-rajash-cha nakhair-alidha-murtiman.
Hridayam yogi-sadhyash-cha nivasam patu me Harih. || 6 ||

Madhyam patu Hiranyaksha-vakshah-kuta-vidaranah.
Nabhim me patu Vishvambharo divya-simho vibhutidah. || 7 ||

Brahmanda-kotayah katyam yasyodara-gatah krishah.
Sa me katim patu nityam divya-simho mahabalah. || 8 ||

Uru me patu Bhadra-shri januni Narakantakah.
Janghe patu Dharadhisho padau me bhakta-vatsalah. || 9 ||

Sarvato-apy-akhilam durgam Nrisimhah patu sarvada.
Atmanah sakalan kleshan hanti sarvatra sarvada. || 10 ||

Imam Nrisimha-kavacham Prahlada-mukha-manditam.
Bhaktiman yah pathen-nityam sarva-papaih pramuchyate. || 11 ||

Putravan dhanavan loke dirghayur-upajayate.
Kamayate yam yam kamam tam tam prapnoty-asanshayam. || 12 ||

Sarvatra jayam-apnoti sarvatra vijayam labhet.
Bhumy-antariksha-divanam grahanam vinivaranam. || 13 ||

|| Iti Shri Brahmanda Purane Prahladoktam Shri Narasimha Kavacham Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
श्री नारद ने कहा — इन्द्रियों का स्वामी मन है, मन का स्वामी प्राणवायु है, प्राणवायु के स्वामी नृसिंह भगवान मेरी गति हों॥ १॥

लोक रक्षा के लिए प्रकट हुए श्री नृसिंह मेरे शिर की रक्षा करें। सर्वव्यापी होकर भी स्तम्भ में निवास करने वाले मेरे ललाट की रक्षा करें। सोम, सूर्य और अग्नि जिनके नेत्र हैं वे नृसिंह मेरे नेत्रों की रक्षा करें। मुनिश्रेष्ठों की स्तुति में प्रसन्न होने वाले नृहरि मेरी स्मृति की रक्षा करें॥ २-३॥

सिंह के समान नासिका वाले मेरी नाक की, लक्ष्मी के मुख प्रिय मेरे मुख की, सर्व विद्याओं के अधिपति नृसिंह मेरी रसना की रक्षा करें॥ ४॥

हिरण्यकशिपु के वक्ष को विदारण करने वाले मेरे मध्य भाग की, विश्वम्भर दिव्य सिंह मेरी नाभि की रक्षा करें। ब्रह्माण्ड कोटियाँ जिनके उदर में समायी हैं वे महाबली दिव्य सिंह मेरी कटि की सदा रक्षा करें॥ ७-८॥

इस प्रह्लाद मुख से सुशोभित नृसिंह कवच को जो भक्तिपूर्वक पढ़ता है वह सर्व पापों से मुक्त होता है, पुत्रवान, धनवान, दीर्घायु होता है और सब कामनाएँ पूर्ण होती हैं। सर्वत्र जय और विजय प्राप्त होती है।

viniyog.shloka: ॐ अस्य श्रीनरसिंहकवचस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य नारद ऋषिः। अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। श्रीनरसिंहो देवता। ॐ नृसिंहाय नमः बीजम्। लक्ष्मी शक्तिः। श्रीनरसिंहप्रीत्यर्थे नरसिंहकवचजपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Provides fierce protection from Lord Narasimha
Traditionally recited for removal of all enemies and evil forces
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of sins and grants purity
Invokes the blessing of sons, wealth, and long life
Traditionally recited for fulfillment of desires without doubt
Invokes victory everywhere in all endeavors
Traditionally invoked for protection from malefic planetary influences
description: The Narasimha Kavacham, known in Sanskrit as नरसिंह कवचम्, is a divine armor prayer for protection in praise of Lord Narasimha. Drawn from Brahmanda Purana (Prahlada-uktam), this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Regular recitation is believed to protect from malefic planetary influences, grant victory everywhere in all endeavors, and remove all sins and grant purity. It is especially recommended on Thursday (Guruvar), Sunday (Ravivar), and Saturday (Shanivar) and during Narasimha Jayanti for maximum spiritual benefit. With 13 verses and a reading time of about 12 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Narasimha Kavacham in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Narasimha Kavacham in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning and transliteration. Powerful protection prayer to Lord Narasimha from Brahmanda Purana.
relatedSearches: Ugra Narasimha vs Lakshmi Narasimha forms
Narasimha mantra for protection from enemies
Sade Sati graha dosha Narasimha kavach upay

seoFaqs.question: What is the Narasimha Kavacham and which Purana preserves it?
seoFaqs.answer: Narasimha Kavacham (नरसिंह कवचम्) is a fierce armour-prayer (kavacha) to Lord Narasimha — the man-lion fourth avatara of Vishnu — preserved in the Brahmanda Purana and traditionally said to have been spoken first by the boy-bhakta Prahlada (Prahlada-uktam). It is therefore one of the oldest Vaishnava protection-stotras, dated by tradition to the satya-yuga of the Hiranyakashipu narrative. The text contains 13 verses with a complete viniyog declaring Narada as the rishi, Anushtup as the chhandas, Sri Narasimha as the devata, the bija 'Om Nrisimhaya Namah' and Lakshmi as the shakti. It is structured as a head-to-toe anga-rakshana — naming Narasimha as the protector of each part of the body from the head (shirah) to the feet (padau). (Sources: Brahmanda Purana; Narasimha Tapani Upanishad tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of the opening Indriyanam Manonatho verse?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 1 establishes a profound philosophical chain: 'Indriyanam mano-natho mano-nathas-tu marutah, marutasya prana-natho Nrisimho me gatir-bhavet' — 'The senses' lord is the mind; the mind's lord is prana (vital wind); prana's lord is Lord Narasimha — may he be my refuge'. This is a compressed yoga-philosophy statement: the senses are unreliable without the mind to govern them; the mind is restless without the prana that animates it; and the prana itself depends on Narasimha as its ultimate source. The verse therefore inserts Narasimha at the very root of the perceptual-cognitive-vital chain — making the kavacha not merely an external armour but an internal recognition that the very life-energy by which we perceive is the Lord. The same metaphysics underlies the Katha Upanishad's chariot analogy. (Sources: Brahmanda Purana; classical Vaishnava commentaries.)

seoFaqs.question: Why does Narasimha appear as a man-lion form?
seoFaqs.answer: The Narasimha avatara was taken specifically to circumvent Hiranyakashipu's near-invincibility boon from Brahma — that he could not be killed by man or beast (na narair na pashubhih), inside or outside (na grihe na bahye), by day or night (na ahani na ratrau), on earth or sky (na bhumau na ambare), by any weapon (na astrair na shastraih). Vishnu therefore manifested as Narasimha — neither fully man nor fully beast (man-lion), at twilight sandhya (neither day nor night), on the threshold (neither inside nor outside), placed Hiranyakashipu on his lap (neither earth nor sky), and used his nails (na astra na shastra). Verse 2 of the Kavacha refers to this: 'lokarakshartha-sambhavah' — manifested for the protection of the worlds; 'stambha-vasah' — dwelling in the pillar from which he emerged. This narrative establishes Narasimha as the deity who breaks all impossible-seeming limitations. (Sources: Bhagavata Purana 7.8; Brahmanda Purana; Vishnu Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: How does the head-to-toe anga-rakshana of the kavacha work?
seoFaqs.answer: Verses 2-9 of the Narasimha Kavacha sequentially install Narasimha as the protector of each body part: head (shirah) — protected by 'lokaraksharthasambhavah' Narasimha; forehead (phala) — protected by stambha-vasi (pillar-dwelling); eyes (drishau) — by Soma-Surya-Agni-lochana (whose three eyes are sun-moon-fire); memory (smriti) — by Nrihari beloved of muni-stutis; nose (nasa) — by Simha-nasa (lion-nosed); mouth — by Lakshmi-mukha-priya; tongue — by sarva-vidyadhipah (lord of all knowledge); hands — by Devaraja with sharp nails; heart — by yogi-sadhya; chest (madhya) — by Hiranyaksha-vaksha-vidarana; navel — by Vishvambhara; waist (kati) — by maha-bala dispelling crores of brahmandas; thighs (uru) — by Bhadra-Shri; knees — by Naraka-antaka; legs (janghe) — by Dharadhisha; feet (padau) — by bhakta-vatsala. Verse 10 concludes that Narasimha protects sarvato-api akhilam durgam — completely on every side. (Sources: Brahmanda Purana; classical Vaishnava paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should the Narasimha Kavacham be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: Narasimha Kavacha is suitable for daily morning recitation as a foundational protection prayer. Days: Thursday (Guruvar — Vishnu-day), Sunday (Ravivar — for fierce-protection deities), Saturday (Shanivar — for graha protection). Festival days: Narasimha Jayanti (Vaishakh Shukla Chaturdashi — the principal festival, fasting throughout the day and Kavacha recitation 1, 3, 9 or 108 times); Holika Dahan night (because it is the immediate prelude to Hiranyakashipu's death); each Ekadashi; Vaikuntha Ekadashi. Special occasions: before any dangerous undertaking, court case, surgery, examination, or when feeling threatened by enemies, evil eye, abhichara (black magic) or graha-dosha. The kavacha is best recited after a bath, facing east, with a tulsi-mala for japa. The bija 'Om Nrisimhaya Namah' may be japaped 108 times before the kavacha. (Sources: Brahmanda Purana; classical Vaishnava paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the significance of the Kavacha being Prahlada-uktam?
seoFaqs.answer: The colophon of the Narasimha Kavacha specifically attributes it as 'Prahlada-uktam' — spoken by Prahlada himself, transmitted via Sage Narada. This attribution carries enormous theological weight in the Vaishnava tradition. Prahlada is not just any devotee but the param-bhagavata — the supreme devotee — born even to a demonic father, immune to poison, fire, mountain-fall, sea and elephants, who finally summoned Narasimha himself by his unwavering bhakti. Verse 11 explicitly names 'Prahlada-mukha-manditam' — 'beautified by the mouth of Prahlada' — and verse 12 promises that whoever recites this kavacha receives putra (sons), dhana (wealth), dirghayuh (long life) and fulfils every desire. Reciting Prahlada's own kavacha is therefore equivalent to receiving Prahlada's protection — being placed within Narasimha's embrace as Prahlada was. (Sources: Brahmanda Purana; Bhagavata Purana 7; Hari-bhakti-vilasa.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Narasimha Kavacha bestow according to the phalashruti?
seoFaqs.answer: Verses 11-13 deliver an extensive phalashruti: (a) sarva-papaih pramuchyate — freed from all sins; (b) putravan dhanavan loke — endowed with sons and wealth in the world; (c) dirghayuh — long life; (d) kamayate yam yam kamam tam tam prapnoti — every desired wish is fulfilled, asanshayam (without doubt); (e) sarvatra jayam-apnoti — victory in every place; (f) sarvatra vijayam labhet — supreme triumph in all undertakings; (g) bhumy-antariksha-divanam grahanam vinivaranam — neutralisation of malefic influences from earth-grahas, sky-grahas and celestial grahas; (h) sarvatra sarvada — complete protection in all places at all times; (i) atmanah sakalan kleshan hanti — destroys every internal klesha (affliction). Traditional Vaishnava lineages add: protection from black magic, abhichara, krityaprayoga, ghosts, untimely death, fire, drowning and weapons. (Sources: Narasimha Kavacha phalashruti; Brahmanda Purana.)
